Executive Threat Intelligence Report

This report groups findings by software and highlights the highest-risk CVEs, mapped threats, likely scenarios, and recommended mitigations.

Executive Summary

The scan identified 16 CVEs across 1 software item. Severity breakdown – Critical: 2, High: 10, Medium: 2, Low: 0.

Key risk indicators:

  • 3 CVEs are listed in the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
  • 2 have publicly available exploit code
  • Highest weighted risk score: 82.7/100

The most prevalent ATT&CK techniques are:

  • Exploit Public-Facing Application (T1190) – observed in 4 CVEs. Tactics: —. D3FEND mitigations: User Geolocation Logon Pattern Analysis, Protocol Metadata Anomaly Detection, Client-server Payload Profiling (+14 more). NIST controls: AU-2 Event Logging, CA-7 Continuous Monitoring, CM-7 Least Functionality (+6 more).
  • Proxy (T1090) – observed in 3 CVEs. Tactics: —. D3FEND mitigations: User Geolocation Logon Pattern Analysis, Protocol Metadata Anomaly Detection, Client-server Payload Profiling (+14 more). NIST controls: AU-2 Event Logging, CA-7 Continuous Monitoring, CM-7 Least Functionality (+6 more).
  • Exploitation for Client Execution (T1203) – observed in 2 CVEs. Tactics: —. D3FEND mitigations: Memory Boundary Tracking, Process Code Segment Verification, Shadow Stack Comparisons (+18 more). NIST controls: AC-3 Access Enforcement, AU-2 Event Logging, CM-7 Least Functionality (+7 more).
  • Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism (T1548) – observed in 2 CVEs. Tactics: —. D3FEND mitigations: Configuration Inventory, Access Modeling, Data Inventory (+32 more). NIST controls: AC-3 Access Enforcement, AC-6 Least Privilege, AU-2 Event Logging (+2 more).
  • Command and Scripting Interpreter (T1059) – observed in 1 CVEs. Tactics: —. D3FEND mitigations: Memory Boundary Tracking, Process Code Segment Verification, Shadow Stack Comparisons (+18 more). NIST controls: AC-3 Access Enforcement, AU-2 Event Logging, CM-7 Least Functionality (+5 more).

Focusing remediation on the mitigations listed above will address the majority of the attack surface revealed by this assessment.

SMA1000 SMA1000

  • Highest Risk CVE Score: 82.7

CIRCL CVE Search returned enrichment data for 16 CVE(s). No active GreyNoise exploitation signals detected at time of scan.

Top CVEs by Risk Score

CVE-2022-0847

  • Risk Score: 82.7 (CRITICAL)
  • CVSS: 7.8 (HIGH)
  • Description: A flaw was found in the way the "flags" member of the new pipe buffer structure was lacking proper initialization in copy_page_to_iter_pipe and push_pipe functions in the Linux kernel and could thus contain stale values. An unprivileged local user could use this flaw to write to pages in the page cache backed by read only files and as such escalate their privileges on the system.
  • KEV: Yes
  • Public Exploit: Yes
  • EPSS: 81.63%
  • Version Confirmed: Unverified
  • Published: 2022-03-10
  • Exploit Sources: NVD refs
  • ATT&CK Techniques: None
  • D3FEND Countermeasures: None
  • NIST 800-53 Controls: None
  • NVD: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2022-0847

CVE-2025-23006

  • Risk Score: 73.9 (HIGH)
  • CVSS: 9.8 (CRITICAL)
  • Description: Pre-authentication deserialization of untrusted data vulnerability has been identified in the SMA1000 Appliance Management Console (AMC) and Central Management Console (CMC), which in specific conditions could potentially enable a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary OS commands.
  • KEV: Yes
  • Public Exploit: No
  • EPSS: 58.50%
  • Version Confirmed: Unverified
  • Published: 2025-01-23
  • Exploit Sources: None
  • ATT&CK Techniques: T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution); T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)
  • D3FEND Countermeasures: Memory Boundary Tracking; Process Code Segment Verification; Shadow Stack Comparisons; Process Segment Execution Prevention; Segment Address Offset Randomization; Stack Frame Canary Validation; File Analysis; File Integrity Monitoring; Dynamic Analysis; Emulated File Analysis; File Eviction; Decoy File; File Encryption; Restore File; Content Filtering; Local File Permissions; Remote File Access Mediation; Content Quarantine; Content Modification; Executable Allowlisting; Executable Denylisting
  • NIST 800-53 Controls: AC-3 Access Enforcement; AU-2 Event Logging; CM-7 Least Functionality; RA-5 Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning; SC-7 Boundary Protection; SI-2 Flaw Remediation; SI-3 Malicious Code Protection; SI-4 System Monitoring
  • NVD: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-23006

CVE-2023-0126

  • Risk Score: 54.5 (MEDIUM)
  • CVSS: 7.5 (HIGH)
  • Description: Pre-authentication path traversal vulnerability in SMA1000 firmware version 12.4.2, which allows an unauthenticated attacker to access arbitrary files and directories stored outside the web root directory.
  • KEV: No
  • Public Exploit: No
  • EPSS: 93.03%
  • Version Confirmed: Unverified
  • Published: 2023-01-19
  • Exploit Sources: None
  • ATT&CK Techniques: T1083 (File and Directory Discovery); T1005 (Data from Local System)

CVE-2021-33909

  • Risk Score: 42.9 (MEDIUM)
  • CVSS: 7.8 (HIGH)
  • Description: fs/seq_file.c in the Linux kernel 3.16 through 5.13.x before 5.13.4 does not properly restrict seq buffer allocations, leading to an integer overflow, an Out-of-bounds Write, and escalation to root by an unprivileged user, aka CID-8cae8cd89f05.
  • KEV: No
  • Public Exploit: Yes
  • EPSS: 2.22%
  • Version Confirmed: Unverified
  • Published: 2021-07-20
  • Exploit Sources: NVD refs
  • ATT&CK Techniques: T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution); T1068 (Exploitation for Privilege Escalation)
  • D3FEND Countermeasures: Memory Boundary Tracking; Process Code Segment Verification; Shadow Stack Comparisons; Process Segment Execution Prevention; Segment Address Offset Randomization; Stack Frame Canary Validation
  • NIST 800-53 Controls: AC-6 Least Privilege; AU-2 Event Logging; CM-6 Configuration Settings; CM-7 Least Functionality; RA-5 Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning; SC-7 Boundary Protection; SI-2 Flaw Remediation; SI-3 Malicious Code Protection; SI-4 System Monitoring
  • NVD: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2021-33909

Threat Scenarios

Scenario 1: Exploitation for Client Execution

  • CVE: CVE-2025-23006
  • ATT&CK Technique: T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution)
  • Narrative: CVE-2025-23006 in SMA1000 is associated with MITRE ATT&CK technique T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution), which falls under the following tactic category: Execution. Exploitation of this vulnerability could lead to the following impacts:
  • Execution: An adversary running malicious code on a local or remote system, potentially leading to payload delivery, data manipulation, system compromise, or further propagation of the attack.
  • Tactics: Execution
  • Impact: Execution: An adversary running malicious code on a local or remote system, potentially leading to payload delivery, data manipulation, system compromise, or further propagation of the attack.

Primary Mitigations:

D3FEND Countermeasures

  • Memory Boundary Tracking; Process Code Segment Verification; Shadow Stack Comparisons; Process Segment Execution Prevention; Segment Address Offset Randomization; Stack Frame Canary Validation; File Analysis; File Integrity Monitoring; Dynamic Analysis; Emulated File Analysis; File Eviction; Decoy File; File Encryption; Restore File; Content Filtering; Local File Permissions; Remote File Access Mediation; Content Quarantine; Content Modification; Executable Allowlisting; Executable Denylisting

NIST 800-53 Controls

AC-3 Access Enforcement; AU-2 Event Logging; CM-7 Least Functionality; RA-5 Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning; SC-7 Boundary Protection; SI-2 Flaw Remediation; SI-3 Malicious Code Protection; SI-4 System Monitoring

==============================================================================

Scenario 2: File and Directory Discovery

  • CVE: CVE-2023-0126
  • ATT&CK Technique: T1083 (File and Directory Discovery)
  • Narrative: An attacker exploits CVE-2023-0126 in SMA1000 to perform ATT&CK technique T1083 (File and Directory Discovery), creating a path to compromise, disruption, or data loss.

Primary Mitigations:

D3FEND Countermeasures

  • File Analysis; File Integrity Monitoring; File Eviction; Decoy File; File Encryption; Restore File; Local File Permissions; Content Filtering; Remote File Access Mediation; Content Quarantine; Content Modification

NIST 800-53 Controls

AC-3 Access Enforcement; AC-6 Least Privilege; AU-2 Event Logging; SC-28 Protection of Information at Rest; SI-4 System Monitoring

==============================================================================

Scenario 3: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism

  • CVE: CVE-2025-40602
  • ATT&CK Technique: T1548 (Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism)
  • Narrative: CVE-2025-40602 in SMA1000 is associated with MITRE ATT&CK technique T1548 (Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism), which falls under the following tactic categories: Privilege Escalation, Defense Evasion. Exploitation of this vulnerability could lead to the following impacts:
  • Privilege Escalation: An adversary gaining higher-level permissions on a system or network, potentially achieving administrative or root-level control, enabling unrestricted access to sensitive resources and system configurations.
  • Defense Evasion: An adversary avoiding detection throughout the course of their intrusion, undermining security monitoring, disabling protections, and reducing the effectiveness of incident response capabilities.
  • Tactics: Privilege Escalation, Defense Evasion
  • Impact: Privilege Escalation: An adversary gaining higher-level permissions on a system or network, potentially achieving administrative or root-level control, enabling unrestricted access to sensitive resources and system configurations.

Defense Evasion: An adversary avoiding detection throughout the course of their intrusion, undermining security monitoring, disabling protections, and reducing the effectiveness of incident response capabilities.

Primary Mitigations:

D3FEND Countermeasures

  • Configuration Inventory; Access Modeling; Data Inventory; Network Traffic Policy Mapping; Application Exception Monitoring; System Call Analysis; System File Analysis; File Analysis; File Integrity Monitoring; Operational Process Monitoring; Dynamic Analysis; Emulated File Analysis; Process Spawn Analysis; File Eviction; Account Locking; Decoy File; File Encryption; Agent Authentication; Change Default Password; System Configuration Permissions; Restore Configuration; Restore File; Unlock Account; Restore User Account Access; Restore Database; System Call Filtering; Content Filtering; Local File Permissions; Remote File Access Mediation; Content Quarantine; Content Modification; User Account Permissions; Executable Allowlisting; Executable Denylisting; Hardware-based Process Isolation

NIST 800-53 Controls

AC-3 Access Enforcement; AC-6 Least Privilege; AU-2 Event Logging; CM-6 Configuration Settings; SI-4 System Monitoring

==============================================================================

  • CVE: CVE-2021-33909
  • ATT&CK Technique: T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution)
  • Narrative: CVE-2021-33909 in SMA1000 is associated with MITRE ATT&CK technique T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution), which falls under the following tactic category: Execution. Exploitation of this vulnerability could lead to the following impacts:
  • Execution: An adversary running malicious code on a local or remote system, potentially leading to payload delivery, data manipulation, system compromise, or further propagation of the attack.

  • File Integrity Monitoring

  • Emulated File Analysis

NIST 800-53

  • AC-3 Access Enforcement
  • AU-2 Event Logging
  • CM-7 Least Functionality
  • RA-5 Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
  • SC-7 Boundary Protection
  • SI-2 Flaw Remediation
  • SI-3 Malicious Code Protection
  • SI-4 System Monitoring
  • AC-6 Least Privilege
  • SC-28 Protection of Information at Rest

Conclusion & Implementation Guidance

This assessment identified 16 CVEs, of which 2 are Critical and 10 are High severity.

3 appear in the CISA KEV catalog; 2 have public exploit code available.

To address the highest-impact attack vectors, apply the following actions in priority order:

Priority 1: Exploit Public-Facing Application (T1190) -- affects 4 CVEs (Tactics: Unknown)

  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: User Geolocation Logon Pattern Analysis
  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Protocol Metadata Anomaly Detection
  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Client-server Payload Profiling
  • ... and 14 additional D3FEND measures
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: AU-2 Event Logging
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: CA-7 Continuous Monitoring
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: CM-7 Least Functionality
  • ... and 6 additional NIST controls

Priority 2: Proxy (T1090) -- affects 3 CVEs (Tactics: Unknown)

  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: User Geolocation Logon Pattern Analysis
  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Protocol Metadata Anomaly Detection
  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Client-server Payload Profiling
  • ... and 14 additional D3FEND measures
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: AU-2 Event Logging
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: CA-7 Continuous Monitoring
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: CM-7 Least Functionality
  • ... and 6 additional NIST controls

Priority 3: Exploitation for Client Execution (T1203) -- affects 2 CVEs (Tactics: Unknown)

  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Memory Boundary Tracking
  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Process Code Segment Verification
  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Shadow Stack Comparisons
  • ... and 18 additional D3FEND measures
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: AC-3 Access Enforcement
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: AU-2 Event Logging
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: CM-7 Least Functionality
  • ... and 7 additional NIST controls

Priority 4: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism (T1548) -- affects 2 CVEs (Tactics: Unknown)

  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Configuration Inventory
  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Access Modeling
  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Data Inventory
  • ... and 32 additional D3FEND measures
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: AC-3 Access Enforcement
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: AC-6 Least Privilege
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: AU-2 Event Logging
  • ... and 2 additional NIST controls

Priority 5: Command and Scripting Interpreter (T1059) -- affects 1 CVE (Tactics: Unknown)

  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Memory Boundary Tracking
  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Process Code Segment Verification
  • Deploy D3FEND counter-measure: Shadow Stack Comparisons
  • ... and 18 additional D3FEND measures
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: AC-3 Access Enforcement
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: AU-2 Event Logging
  • Enact NIST 800-53 control: CM-7 Least Functionality
  • ... and 5 additional NIST controls

Across all findings, 65 unique D3FEND counter-measures and 16 unique NIST 800-53 controls were identified.

Implementation roadmap:

1. Prioritize -- Address KEV-listed and publicly-exploitable CVEs first.

2. Patch -- Apply vendor patches for all version-confirmed CVEs.

3. Harden -- Deploy the D3FEND counter-measures and NIST controls listed above.

4. Validate -- Confirm effectiveness with periodic red-team or ATT&CK-mapping tests.

5. Monitor -- Continuously scan for new CVEs and update the mitigation set.
